identification.question: What term refers to raw facts without context?
What term refers to processed data that reveals meaning?
What is data about data called?
What system manages database structure and controls access to data?
What term describes duplication of data?
What term describes lack of uniformity across data?
What term describes difficulty accessing scattered data across files?
What transaction property means 'all or nothing'?
What type of independence allows file structure changes without affecting data access?
What type of database supports only one user at a time?
What type of database distributes data across multiple sites?
What database supports daily business operations?
What database is used for tactical or strategic decision-making?
What DBMS component stores metadata definitions?
What nonprocedural query language is the de facto standard for relational databases?
What operational-level system captures production data?
What tactical-level system summarizes operational data?
What strategic-level system supports non-routine decisions?
What database design phase identifies entities and relationships?
What term refers to anything about which data is collected?
What term describes how entities are associated?
What rule restricts allowable data values?
Which data model was proposed in 1970 by E. F. Codd?
Which conceptual model was introduced by Chen in 1976?
What type of entity depends on another entity for existence?
What key uniquely identifies each entity instance?
What key references a primary key in another table?
What term defines the minimum and maximum number of related entity occurrences?
What relationship occurs when an entity relates to itself?
What entity type is used to implement M:N relationships?

multipleChoice.question: Which file system component represents a logically connected set of fields forming a single complete entry?
Which file system component is defined as a group of characters with specific meaning?
Which file system component is a collection of related records?
Which dependency occurs when data access changes due to modifications in storage characteristics?
Which database type runs on a personal computer and supports only one user?
Which database type supports multiple users simultaneously within an organization?
Which database location model stores data at a single site?
Which type of data exists in its original raw state (e.g., emails, videos)?
Which type of data includes formats such as XML or JSON?
Which phase of database design focuses on understanding data needs and objectives?
Which phase maps the logical design to actual storage devices?
Which data model uses an upside-down tree structure?
Which data model allows records to have multiple parents?
Which model combines data and behavior into self-contained objects?
Which modeling language is commonly used in object-oriented data modeling?
Which data model is optimized for complex analytical queries in data warehouses?
Which type of database is designed for large-scale, distributed environments?
Which term refers to a user-specific view of data defined by external schemas?
Which abstraction level represents the global organization-wide view of data?
Which abstraction level maps the conceptual design to DBMS structures?
Which type of entity is independent and always has a unique identifier?
Which attribute type must always have a value?
Which attribute type may be left empty?
Which attribute type can be subdivided into smaller components?
Which attribute type cannot be subdivided?
Which key is unique and minimal but not chosen as the primary key?
Which key consists of more than one attribute?
Which participation requires an entity occurrence to have a corresponding related entity?
Which relationship degree involves three entities?
Which entity type acts as a bridge to connect entities in many-to-many relationships and may contain additional attributes?
